reasoning: The patient is conscious, focused, and capable of making decisions.  Competent patients have the right to refuse treatment, including life-saving procedures, even though doing so raises the risk of serious complications, including death.  Unless there is doubt about his capacity, attempts to override his decision—such as getting approval from family members, the courts, or the bioethics committee—are not recommended.  The patient's current autonomous decision is not superseded by reviewing the living will (option F), although it can enhance comprehension.
